revocation_authority.detail: The first signed-node identity surface keeps revocation posture explicit through an epoch-scoped revocation feed with a two-epoch grace window. It does not claim automatic slashing or hardware-backed remote attestation yet.

wallet.detail: The first signed identity retains one operator wallet namespace for `google_l4_validator_node` so settlement and reward accounting can bind to a machine-legible address before on-chain publication exists.

software_attestation.detail: The first public identity layer freezes one deterministic software build digest for `google_l4_validator_node` above the canonical environment key and the retained source authority artifacts.

capability_projection.detail: The capability projection freezes accelerator, backend, network, and storage truth for `google_l4_validator_node` without widening beyond the source contract already admitted by the program manifest.

benchmark_evidence.detail: Google coordinator evidence ties the admitted validator, contributor-window, and checkpoint-writer posture back to the retained two-node swarm contract.
Identity-profile evidence freezes operator role tags and the admitted aggregator-side data-builder seam for the same Google node.

capability_signature.detail: The canonical signed identity uses one deterministic ed25519 testnet signer for `google_l4_validator_node` so admission, validator scoring, and fraud review all point at the same stable payload.

refusal_examples.detail: The Google L4 validator node does not admit dense_full_model_rank in the current source contract, so the signed identity refuses dense-rank claims explicitly instead of letting public admission prose widen the node.

identities.detail: Google private-cloud coordinator node retained as the strongest multi-role public testnet participant: validator, checkpoint authority, contributor-window miner, aggregator, and relay support.

wallet.detail: The first signed identity retains one operator wallet namespace for `runpod_8xh100_dense_node` so settlement and reward accounting can bind to a machine-legible address before on-chain publication exists.

software_attestation.detail: The first public identity layer freezes one deterministic software build digest for `runpod_8xh100_dense_node` above the canonical environment key and the retained source authority artifacts.

capability_projection.detail: The capability projection freezes accelerator, backend, network, and storage truth for `runpod_8xh100_dense_node` without widening beyond the source contract already admitted by the program manifest.

benchmark_evidence.detail: RunPod launch-profile evidence proves the exact eight-H100 dense-rank lane and the attached checkpoint-writer posture retained in the current source contract.
RunPod preflight evidence keeps dense-rank readiness, eval-worker posture, and artifact-building support tied to the retained operator preflight policy.

capability_signature.detail: The canonical signed identity uses one deterministic ed25519 testnet signer for `runpod_8xh100_dense_node` so admission, validator scoring, and fraud review all point at the same stable payload.

refusal_examples.detail: The current decentralized network binds public_miner to validated_contributor_window rather than dense_full_model_rank, so the RunPod dense node refuses public_miner identity claims honestly in this issue.
The RunPod eight-H100 dense node does not admit validator execution in the current source contract, so validator identity claims stay fail-closed here.

identities.detail: RunPod dense node retained as dense compute and public checkpoint sidecar capacity, but not yet as a public contributor-window miner under the current network role binding.

wallet.detail: The first signed identity retains one operator wallet namespace for `local_rtx4080_workstation` so settlement and reward accounting can bind to a machine-legible address before on-chain publication exists.

software_attestation.detail: The first public identity layer freezes one deterministic software build digest for `local_rtx4080_workstation` above the canonical environment key and the retained source authority artifacts.

capability_projection.detail: The capability projection freezes accelerator, backend, network, and storage truth for `local_rtx4080_workstation` without widening beyond the source contract already admitted by the program manifest.

benchmark_evidence.detail: RTX 4080 bring-up evidence keeps contributor-window, eval-worker, and data-builder capability claims tied to the retained trusted-LAN workstation report.

capability_signature.detail: The canonical signed identity uses one deterministic ed25519 testnet signer for `local_rtx4080_workstation` so admission, validator scoring, and fraud review all point at the same stable payload.

refusal_examples.detail: The local RTX 4080 workstation does not admit validator execution in the current source contract, so public_validator admission remains explicitly refused.
The local RTX 4080 workstation does not currently carry checkpoint_writer in the source contract, so checkpoint-authority identity claims remain blocked here.

identities.detail: Local Linux RTX 4080 workstation retained as a contributor-window public miner and aggregator-side support node under operator-managed admission.

wallet.detail: The first signed identity retains one operator wallet namespace for `local_mlx_mac_workstation` so settlement and reward accounting can bind to a machine-legible address before on-chain publication exists.

software_attestation.detail: The first public identity layer freezes one deterministic software build digest for `local_mlx_mac_workstation` above the canonical environment key and the retained source authority artifacts.

capability_projection.detail: The capability projection freezes accelerator, backend, network, and storage truth for `local_mlx_mac_workstation` without widening beyond the source contract already admitted by the program manifest.

benchmark_evidence.detail: MLX bring-up evidence keeps the Apple workstation's dense-rank, contributor-window, validator, and data-builder claims bound to the retained bring-up report on the committed host family.

capability_signature.detail: The canonical signed identity uses one deterministic ed25519 testnet signer for `local_mlx_mac_workstation` so admission, validator scoring, and fraud review all point at the same stable payload.

refusal_examples.detail: The Apple MLX workstation does not admit checkpoint_writer in the current source contract, so checkpoint_authority stays explicitly refused even though the node can validate and aggregate.

identities.detail: Local Apple Silicon workstation retained as a mixed-role testnet node for contributor-window mining, validation, and aggregation, while dense-rank capability remains outside the current public role map.

claim_boundary: This contract set closes one signed public-node identity layer above the canonical compute-source contracts and the decentralized network contract. It binds wallet, software build digest, capability digests, benchmark evidence, admitted roles, admitted execution classes, typed refusals, and revocation posture into one machine-legible surface. It does not claim hardware-backed attestation, on-chain wallet verification, permissionless registration, or automatic slashing.
